title: श्री स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री स्तुति ॥
(श्री वेदान्त देशिक कृत)

मनीषिणामानन्दमापादयन्ती
हरेर्लक्ष्मीमात्रबोधास्पदत्वात्।
वसन्ती श्रीरत्नगर्भा धरित्री
नमस्ते ह्रीपुष्टिकान्तिस्वरूपे॥ १॥

श्रीरङ्गनायकि शुभे श्रितलोकदीपे
भक्तार्तिहारिणि हरिप्रिये मङ्गले।
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलनिवासिनि देवि विष्णो-
र्वक्षःस्थले स्थितसदा तव चाभिवन्दे॥ २॥

समस्तदेवमुनिमानवपूजिताङ्घ्रे
सन्तापहारिणि सदा शुभदे प्रसन्ने।
आलोड्य सागरमपि त्वमुदीयमाना
पद्मासने कमलपत्रविलोचनेऽम्ब॥ ३॥

ईशित्रि सर्वजगतामिह या प्रसिद्धा
लक्ष्मीर्विभोर्हृदयवासिनि चाम्बुजाक्षी।
विश्वस्य मातृपदवीमुपगम्य नित्यं
यस्याः कटाक्षसरणिं सकला स्पृहन्ति॥ ४॥

दारिद्र्यदुःखभयहारिणि कीर्तिवर्धिनि
शान्तिप्रदे प्रणतपालिनि शोभमाने।
कल्याणदे कमलवासिनि विष्णुपत्नि
लक्ष्मि प्रसीद सततं मम जीवनेऽस्मिन्॥ ५॥

यस्यास्तु वीक्षणकलासविलासलीला
मिन्दीवरप्रसवगन्धवहाभिषिक्ता।
सम्पत्तिसौख्यसुभगा सकलासु दिक्षु
तामेव भक्तिनमितो भजामि लक्ष्मीम्॥ ६॥

पद्मालये पद्मपलाशलोचने
पद्मप्रिये पद्मदलायताक्षि।
विश्वप्रिये विष्णुमनोऽनुकूले
त्वत्पादपद्मं मयि सन्निधत्स्व॥ ७॥

मातर्लक्ष्मि नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्तुभ्यं पुनः पुनः।
सर्वदा सर्वदा भक्तिं देहि मे परमेश्वरि॥ ८॥

यत्कटाक्षसमुपासनाविधिं
श्रीमनोऽनुकूलमिह कल्पयन्ति ये।
तेषु देशिकवरो दयां सदा
वर्धयत्यवनतेषु सज्जनेष्वसौ॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री वेदान्तदेशिक कृत श्री स्तुतिः सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Stuti ||
(Shri Vedanta Deshika Krit)

Manishinam-anandam-aapadayanti
Harer-Lakshmim-atra-bodha-aspadatvat |
Vasanti Shrir-atna-garbha dharitri
namas-te hri-pushti-kanti-svarupe || 1 ||

Shriranganaayaki shubhe shrita-loka-deepe
bhakta-arti-haarini Hari-priye mangale |
Shri-Venkatachala-nivasini Devi Vishno-
rvakshah-sthale sthita-sada tava cha-abhivande || 2 ||

Samasta-deva-muni-manava-pujita-anghre
santapa-haarini sada shubhade prasanne |
Alodya saagaram-api tvam-udiyamana
Padmasane kamala-patra-vilochane'mba || 3 ||

Ishitri sarva-jagatam-iha ya prasiddha
Lakshmeer-vibhor-hridaya-vasini cha-ambuja-akshi |
Vishvasya matri-padavim-upagamya nityam
yasyah kataksha-saranim sakala sprihanti || 4 ||

Daridrya-duhkha-bhaya-haarini keerti-vardhini
shanti-prade pranata-palini shobhamane |
Kalyanade kamala-vasini Vishnu-patni
Lakshmi prasida satatam mama jivane'smin || 5 ||

Yasyastu vikshana-kala-savilasa-leela-
m-indivara-prasava-gandha-vaha-abhishikta |
Sampatti-saukhya-subhaga sakalasu dikshu
taam-eva bhakti-namito bhajami Lakshmim || 6 ||

Padmalaye padma-palasha-lochane
padma-priye padma-dala-ayata-akshi |
Vishva-priye Vishnu-mano'nukule
tvat-pada-padmam mayi sannidhatsva || 7 ||

Matar-Lakshmi namas-tubhyam namas-tubhyam punah punah |
Sarvada sarvada bhaktim dehi me Parameshvari || 8 ||

Yat-kataksha-samupasana-vidhim
Shri-mano'nukulam-iha kalpayanti ye |
Teshu Deshika-varo dayam sada
vardhayaty-avateshu sajjaneshu-asau || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Vedanta-Deshika Krit Shri Stutih Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
मनीषियों को आनन्द प्रदान करने वाली, हरि की लक्ष्मी — बोध का आस्पद होने से — रत्नगर्भा धरित्री में वास करती हैं। ह्री, पुष्टि, कान्ति स्वरूपा — आपको नमस्कार!॥ १॥

श्रीरङ्गनायकी, शुभे, आश्रित लोकों की दीपिका, भक्तों की पीड़ा हरने वाली, हरिप्रिया, मंगले — श्रीवेंकटाचल निवासिनी देवी — विष्णु के वक्षःस्थल में सदा स्थित — आपको अभिवन्दन!॥ २॥

समस्त देवता, मुनि, मनुष्यों द्वारा पूजित चरणों वाली, सन्ताप हारिणी, सदा शुभदायिनी, प्रसन्ना — समुद्र मन्थन से उदय हुईं, पद्मासना, कमलपत्र-नेत्रा — हे अम्ब!॥ ३॥

जो समस्त जगत् की ईश्वरी प्रसिद्ध हैं, विभु (विष्णु) के हृदय-वासिनी, कमलनयना — विश्व की माता का पद प्राप्तकर जिनकी कटाक्ष-सरणि सभी चाहते हैं॥ ४॥

दारिद्र्य-दुःख-भय हरने वाली, कीर्तिवर्धिनी, शान्तिप्रदा, प्रणतों की पालिनी — कल्याणदा, कमलवासिनी, विष्णुपत्नी लक्ष्मी — मेरे जीवन में सदा प्रसन्न रहें!॥ ५॥

जिनकी विलास-लीला दृष्टि इन्दीवर पुष्प की सुगन्ध से अभिषिक्त है — सम्पत्ति-सौख्य-सुभग सभी दिशाओं में — उन्हीं लक्ष्मी को भक्तिनत होकर भजता हूँ॥ ६॥

हे पद्मालया, पद्मपलाशलोचना, पद्मप्रिया — विश्वप्रिया, विष्णु-मनोनुकूला — अपने पदपद्म मुझमें स्थापित कीजिए!॥ ७॥

माता लक्ष्मी! आपको बारम्बार नमस्कार! हे परमेश्वरी! सर्वदा मुझे भक्ति दीजिए!॥ ८॥
benefits: Invokes the grace of Goddess Lakshmi through Vedanta Deshika's devotion
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, suffering, and fear
Invokes the blessing of eternal devotion to lakshmi-narayana
Traditionally associated with peace and auspiciousness to life
Foundation prayer of Sri Vaishnava tradition
description: The Sri Stuti, known in Sanskrit as श्री स्तुति, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Goddess Lakshmi. Rooted in Vedanta Deshika - Sri Stuti, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to grant wealth, prosperity, and fame and invoke the grace of Goddess Lakshmi through Vedanta Deshika's devotion. It is also said to remove poverty, suffering, and fear. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Friday (Shukravar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Diwali, Ekadashi, and Sharad Purnima. At 9 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 7 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Sri Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Sri Stuti by Vedanta Deshika in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Hymn to Goddess Lakshmi from Sri Vaishnava tradition.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sri Stuti and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sri Stuti is a celebrated Sanskrit hymn of nine verses dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi (Sri), composed by Sri Vedanta Desika (1268-1369 CE), the eminent Sri Vaishnava acharya, philosopher and poet who succeeded Sri Ramanujacharya as the principal exponent of Vishishtadvaita Vedanta in the Vadagalai tradition. Vedanta Desika (also called Venkatanatha) composed the Sri Stuti at Tirupati after a young brahmin in poverty came to him for help, and tradition holds that immediately after the recitation a shower of gold coins fell from the sky. The hymn is part of his celebrated Stotra Bhushana collection of twenty-eight stotras and is among the most widely recited Lakshmi prayers in the Sri Vaishnava tradition. (Sources: Vedanta Desika hagiography; Hindupedia Vedanta Desika; Sri Vaishnava sampradaya texts.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Sri Stuti describe Goddess Lakshmi?
seoFaqs.answer: Vedanta Desika invokes Lakshmi under her supreme Sri Vaishnava aspects: Manishinam-anandam-apadayanti (the bestower of bliss to the wise), Hari-Lakshmi (the eternal consort of Hari-Vishnu), Hri-pushti-kanti-svarupa (the form of modesty, nourishment and radiance), Sri Ranganayaki (the queen of Srirangam, presiding deity of the Sri Ranganatha temple), Sri Venkatachala-nivasini (resident of the Tirumala-Tirupati hills as Padmavati-Alamelumanga, consort of Lord Venkateshwara), Vishnoh vakshah-sthale sthita-sada (eternally seated on Vishnu's chest as Sri-vatsa), Padmasana (lotus-throned), Padmalaya (lotus-abode), Padma-palasha-lochana (lotus-petal-eyed), and Vishnu-mano-anukula (one in tune with Vishnu's heart). (Sources: Sri Stuti verses 1-7; Sri Vaishnava commentary tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the central theological teaching of Sri Stuti in Sri Vaishnavism?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Stuti embodies the central Sri Vaishnava doctrine of purushakara — that Lakshmi serves as the divine mediator (purushakara-bhuta) who recommends the devotee to Lord Narayana for moksha. Verse 4 states Ishitri sarva-jagatam — Lakshmi is the supreme ruler of all worlds, and her kataksha (sidelong glance of grace) is sought by all because she opens the path to Vishnu's mercy. The Sri Vaishnava sampradaya holds that no jiva can approach Narayana directly without first obtaining Sri's grace; she alone has the unconditional compassion (nirhetuka-kripa) to advocate for even the most fallen souls. This dual-divinity Lakshmi-Narayana theology distinguishes Sri Vaishnavism from other Vaishnava schools and makes Sri Stuti a foundational text. (Sources: Sri Bhashya; Vedanta Desika Rahasya texts; Hindupedia Sri Vaishnavism.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Sri Stuti be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Friday (Shukravar) is the principal weekday for Lakshmi recitations, and Thursday (Guruvar) is also auspicious for Vishnu-Lakshmi worship. Sri Stuti is especially recited during Diwali (Lakshmi Puja on Kartika Amavasya), Sharad Purnima (Kojagara — when Lakshmi descends to bless those awake), Varalakshmi Vratam (Sravana Shukla Pakshya Friday before full moon), Ekadashi tithis (sacred to Vishnu and Lakshmi), Akshaya Tritiya (Vaisakha Shukla Tritiya — celebrated as supreme prosperity day), Dhanteras (the start of Diwali week), and during the entire Margashirsha month at Sri Vaishnava temples (especially during the Adhyayana Utsava at Srirangam and Tirupati). It is also the standard daily recitation for Sri Vaishnava brahmacharis, grihasthas and sannyasis. (Sources: Drikpanchang Lakshmi Puja; Sri Vaishnava temple calendars.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Sri Stuti grant to devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition (rooted in the gold-shower miracle at Vedanta Desika's recitation) declares that Sri Stuti recitation grants daridrya-duhkha-bhaya-haarini — removal of poverty, sorrow and fear (verse 5 itself addresses Lakshmi as such); kirti-vardhini (increase of fame); shanti-prada (grant of peace); kalyana-prada (auspiciousness); and ultimately param vidyam shashvatim — the supreme eternal knowledge of moksha through Lakshmi's purushakara-grace. Devotees report particular benefit in financial difficulties, business reversals, debt liquidation, career stagnation, and matters requiring rajya-lakshmi (royal fortune). For spiritual aspirants it is the foundational prayer for sharanagati (surrender) since only Lakshmi can grant the qualification to surrender to Narayana. (Sources: Sri Stuti verses; Vedanta Desika hagiography; Sri Vaishnava devotional tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Sri Stuti differ from Lakshmi Ashtottara, Kanakadhara Stotra and Sri Suktam?
seoFaqs.answer: All four are principal Lakshmi recitations of varying provenance and emphasis. Sri Stuti by Vedanta Desika (9 verses, 14th century, Sri Vaishnava) emphasises Lakshmi's purushakara role and her presence at Srirangam and Tirupati. Lakshmi Ashtottara Shata Namavali (108 names) is used for daily archana with kumkum and lotus flowers. Kanakadhara Stotra by Adi Shankaracharya (22 verses, 8th century, Smarta) is the celebrated gold-shower hymn composed at the impoverished brahmin's house in Kalady. Sri Suktam from the Rig Vedic khilani (16 verses) is the most ancient Vedic Lakshmi hymn used in shrauta and grihya rituals including Sri Lakshmi-puja and Lakshmi-homam. The Sri Stuti is the Sri Vaishnava sampradaya's primary daily Lakshmi text. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Lakshmi collection; Sri Vaishnava sampradaya.)
